**Analyzing Business Requirements**

I'm now focusing on specific features for the React app. I've designed the reports to feature sales and profit margin charts. The settings will handle JSONBin configuration. I'm finalizing data structure with TypeScript interfaces. This includes Product, Transaction, and Expense interfaces, plus the overall AppData structure for JSONBin. I'm refining my ideas as the technical development evolves.


**Exploring AI Integrations**

I'm now integrating AI insights. I'm thinking of a margin % display when setting sale prices. I'll utilize Gemini to offer restocking suggestions or pinpoint bestsellers via a "Business Report". The goal is to generate insights from the raw JSON data.

I'm considering the JSONBin API Key requirement for the storage service. I've designed the foundational structure for the React app including index.html, App.tsx and other related files. I'm focusing on defining the file structure, including components, pages, services, constants and types.


**Implementing Data Storage**

I'm now focusing on connecting to data storage. I'll allow configuration via a Settings tab, or use a default. `localStorage` will serve as a fallback. I'm exploring a `DataProvider` context for state management, which seems best for managing async loading/saving.

I'm designing the UI with Tailwind utility classes. I'm tracking "Purchase" and "Sale" transactions to differentiate between wholesaler and customer. The POS will manage discounts (percentage or fixed) and payment methods with a dropdown. The app will work.


**Formulating AI Advice**

I'm developing an "AI Advisor" feature to enhance pricing intelligence. I'm leveraging inventory and sales history to provide actionable advice. Using the `gemini-3-flash-preview` model, I'll alert users to low stock levels and potential margin issues. I'm integrating this functionality within the Inventory "Add/Edit" modal to offer real-time pricing guidance.


**Enhancing UI and Data**

I'm now integrating a margin percentage indicator into the `Sell Price` input field; a visual warning will appear if the margin falls below a set threshold. I'm also addressing potential JSONBin rate limits by implementing debouncing and limiting saves to specific events, assuming single-user access for now. I'm using `gemini-3-flash-preview` for quick analysis. I've chosen a design theme of white, soft grays, and a teal accent to reflect the skincare focus. Additionally, I have the final code structure in mind.


**Defining App Architecture**

I'm now focusing on ensuring that the `recharts` and `lucide-react` libraries are used correctly and efficiently. My `App.tsx` file will handle initial data loading, defaulting to an empty structure if no data is found. The design incorporates a settings area to manage JSONBin access with a fallback to LocalStorage, for testing and immediate use. Finally, I'm designing an "AI Insight" feature on the dashboard for actionable advice via Gemini, based on inventory and recent sales data.

import React from 'react'; import { BookOpen, Package, ShoppingCart, TrendingUp, Settings, Shield, Server, Monitor, AlertTriangle } from 'lucide-react'; const Documentation: React.FC = () => { return (

System Documentation

Official guide and technical reference for LinenLogic - Skincare Towel Business Manager.

{/* 1. Overview */}

1. Project Overview

LinenLogic is a comprehensive progressive web application designed to manage the end-to-end operations of a specialized skincare towel business in Ghana. It moves beyond simple spreadsheets by integrating inventory tracking, point-of-sale (POS) processing, expense management, and AI-driven business intelligence into a single platform.

The system is built to handle both wholesale and retail transactions, catering to the specific needs of managing stock levels, calculating real-time profit margins, and providing insights based on local market trends.

{/* 2. Key Modules */}

2. User Manual & Key Modules

Inventory Management

  • Product CRUD: Create, Read, Update, and Delete towel products with details like Cost Price, Sell Price, and Stock Level.
  • Smart Pricing: The system automatically suggests a sell price (1.4x markup) when adding items.
  • Bulk Editing: Select multiple items to update prices by percentage or change categories in one go.
  • Restock Workflow: Use the dedicated "Restock" button to add inventory without overriding existing data, automatically calculating new weighted costs if needed.
  • Stale Price Alert: A visual clock icon warns if a price hasn't been updated in 6 months.

Point of Sale (POS)

  • Cart System: Search and add products to a temporary cart. Supports quantity adjustments and manual discounts.
  • Checkout: Records a transaction, deducts stock immediately, and logs revenue/profit.
  • Sales History: View past transactions. Includes a date range filter to analyze specific periods (e.g., "Last Week").
  • Voiding: Transactions can be voided to reverse revenue and restore stock levels automatically.

Dashboard & Analytics

  • Time-Based Filtering: Toggle between 2 Weeks, 1 Month, 1 Quarter, or 1 Year views.
  • Financial Metrics: Real-time calculation of Net Profit (Revenue - Cost of Goods - Expenses).
  • Inventory Valuation: Tracks total capital tied up in stock vs. potential revenue.
  • Charts: Visual bar charts showing sales trends over the selected period.

Configuration

  • Security PIN: Protects critical settings (API keys, Data Export) from unauthorized access.
  • Categories: dynamic management of product and expense categories.
  • Wireframe Mode: A specialized "Low-Fi" toggle for generating blueprint-style screenshots for documentation/prototyping.
{/* 3. Technical Architecture */}

3. Technical Architecture

Frontend Stack

  • React 19 (Functional Components)
  • TypeScript (Strict Typing)
  • Tailwind CSS (Styling System)
  • Lucide React (Iconography)
  • Recharts (Data Visualization)

Data Persistence

  • Primary: Browser LocalStorage (Offline first).
  • Cloud Sync: JSONBin.io integration for cross-device backup (configurable in Settings).
  • State Management: React Hooks (useState, useMemo, useEffect).

Security & AI

  • Auth: SHA-256 Hashing for Admin PIN protection.
  • AI Engine: Google Gemini API (Flash 2.5/3 model) for business logic analysis and generating reports.
{/* 4. Design Thinking */}

4. UX/UI Design Philosophy

The application was designed with a "Mobile-First, Data-Dense" philosophy. Recognizing that shop owners may use tablets or phones on the floor, the interface uses large touch targets for POS actions while reserving dense tabular data views for the Inventory and History tabs.

Color Theory: A primary Teal palette (#0d9488) was chosen to evoke cleanliness and calm, aligning with the skincare/towel industry. Red/Green indicators are used strictly for financial health (Profit/Loss) and inventory alerts (Low Stock).

Prototyping Tooling: The built-in Wireframe Mode allows the developer to strip away aesthetic bias during stakeholder presentations, focusing the conversation on layout and information architecture rather than color choices.

{/* --- DOCUMENT CONTENT --- */}
{/* 1. Overview */}

1. Project Overview

LinenLogic is a comprehensive progressive web application designed to manage the end-to-end operations of a specialized skincare towel business in Ghana. It moves beyond simple spreadsheets by integrating inventory tracking, point-of-sale (POS) processing, expense management, and AI-driven business intelligence into a single platform.

The system is built to handle both wholesale and retail transactions, catering to the specific needs of managing stock levels, calculating real-time profit margins, and providing insights based on local market trends.

{/* 2. Key Modules */}

2. User Manual & Key Modules

Inventory Management

  • Product CRUD: Create, Read, Update, and Delete towel products with details like Cost Price, Sell Price, and Stock Level.
  • Smart Pricing: The system automatically suggests a sell price (1.4x markup) when adding items.
  • Bulk Editing: Select multiple items to update prices by percentage or change categories in one go.
  • Restock Workflow: Use the dedicated "Restock" button to add inventory without overriding existing data, automatically calculating new weighted costs if needed.
  • Stale Price Alert: A visual clock icon warns if a price hasn't been updated in 6 months.

Point of Sale (POS)

  • Cart System: Search and add products to a temporary cart. Supports quantity adjustments and manual discounts.
  • Checkout: Records a transaction, deducts stock immediately, and logs revenue/profit.
  • Sales History: View past transactions. Includes a date range filter to analyze specific periods (e.g., "Last Week").
  • Voiding: Transactions can be voided to reverse revenue and restore stock levels automatically.

Dashboard & Analytics

  • Time-Based Filtering: Toggle between 2 Weeks, 1 Month, 1 Quarter, or 1 Year views.
  • Financial Metrics: Real-time calculation of Net Profit (Revenue - Cost of Goods - Expenses).
  • Inventory Valuation: Tracks total capital tied up in stock vs. potential revenue.
  • Charts: Visual bar charts showing sales trends over the selected period.

Configuration

  • Security PIN: Protects critical settings (API keys, Data Export) from unauthorized access.
  • Categories: dynamic management of product and expense categories.
  • Wireframe Mode: A specialized "Low-Fi" toggle for generating blueprint-style screenshots for documentation/prototyping.
{/* 3. Technical Architecture */}

3. Technical Architecture

Frontend Stack

  • React 19 (Functional Components)
  • TypeScript (Strict Typing)
  • Tailwind CSS (Styling System)
  • Lucide React (Iconography)
  • Recharts (Data Visualization)

Data Persistence

  • Primary: Browser LocalStorage (Offline first).
  • Cloud Sync: JSONBin.io integration for cross-device backup (configurable in Settings).
  • State Management: React Hooks (useState, useMemo, useEffect).

Security & AI

  • Auth: SHA-256 Hashing for Admin PIN protection.
  • AI Engine: Google Gemini API (Flash 2.5/3 model) for business logic analysis and generating reports.
{/* 4. Design Thinking */}

4. UX/UI Design Philosophy

The application was designed with a "Mobile-First, Data-Dense" philosophy. Recognizing that shop owners may use tablets or phones on the floor, the interface uses large touch targets for POS actions while reserving dense tabular data views for the Inventory and History tabs.

Color Theory: A primary Teal palette (#0d9488) was chosen to evoke cleanliness and calm, aligning with the skincare/towel industry. Red/Green indicators are used strictly for financial health (Profit/Loss) and inventory alerts (Low Stock).

Prototyping Tooling: The built-in Wireframe Mode allows the developer to strip away aesthetic bias during stakeholder presentations, focusing the conversation on layout and information architecture rather than color choices.

{/* 1. Overview */}

1. Project Overview

LinenLogic is a comprehensive progressive web application designed to manage the end-to-end operations of a specialized skincare towel business in Ghana. It moves beyond simple spreadsheets by integrating inventory tracking, point-of-sale (POS) processing, expense management, and AI-driven business intelligence into a single platform.

Core Value: The system is built to handle both wholesale and retail transactions, catering to the specific needs of managing stock levels, calculating real-time profit margins, and providing insights based on local market trends.

{/* 2. Key Modules */}

2. User Manual & Key Modules

Inventory Management

  • Product CRUD: Create, Read, Update, and Delete towel products with details like Cost Price, Sell Price, and Stock Level.
  • Smart Pricing: The system automatically suggests a sell price (1.4x markup) when adding items.
  • Restock Workflow: Use the dedicated "Restock" button to add inventory without overriding existing data.
  • Stale Price Alert: A visual clock icon warns if a price hasn't been updated in 6 months.

Point of Sale (POS)

  • Cart System: Search and add products to a temporary cart. Supports quantity adjustments and manual discounts.
  • Checkout: Records a transaction, deducts stock immediately, and logs revenue/profit.
  • Sales History: View past transactions with date filtering.
  • Voiding: Reverse transactions to correct errors and restore stock.

Dashboard & Analytics

  • Time-Based Filtering: Toggle between 2 Weeks, 1 Month, 1 Quarter, or 1 Year views.
  • Financial Metrics: Real-time calculation of Net Profit (Revenue - Cost of Goods - Expenses).
  • Inventory Valuation: Tracks total capital tied up in stock vs. potential revenue.

Configuration

  • Security PIN: Protects critical settings (API keys, Data Export) from unauthorized access.
  • Wireframe Mode: A specialized "Low-Fi" toggle for generating blueprint-style screenshots for documentation/prototyping.
{/* 3. Tech Arch */}

3. Technical Architecture

Frontend Stack

Based on your current application architecture, here is a breakdown of exactly what happens if you try to save images directly into **JSONBin.io**, why it is generally discouraged, and the recommended alternative.

### 1. How It Would Work Technically (Base64)
Since JSONBin stores **text (JSON)** and images are **binary files**, you cannot simply "upload" an image to a JSON object. You must convert the image into a text string using **Base64 Encoding**.

Your data structure would change from this:
```json
{
  "id": "p1",
  "name": "Luxury Towel"
}
```
To this:
```json
{
  "id": "p1",
  "name": "Luxury Towel",
  "image": "data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAAAQABAAD/2wBDAAMCAgMCAgMDAwMEAwMEBQgFBQQEBQoHBwYIDAoMDAsKCwsNDhIQDQ4RDgsLEBYQERMUFRUVDA8XGBYUGBIUFRT/..."
}
```

### 2. The Consequences (Why this is dangerous)

If you implement this in `LinenLogic`, you will face immediate critical issues:

#### A. The "Bloat" Factor (33% Size Increase)
Base64 encoding increases the size of a file by approximately **33%**.
*   A **2MB** photo from a phone camera becomes **~2.7MB** of text string.
*   If you have 10 products with images, your JSON file is now **27MB**.

#### B. App Performance Crash
Currently, your `App.tsx` loads **all** data at once:
```typescript
// App.tsx
const loadedData = await loadData(settings); // Downloads the ENTIRE database
```
*   **Loading Time:** The user has to download 27MB+ of data just to open the dashboard. On a 4G connection in Ghana, this could take 30–60 seconds.
*   **UI Freezing:** The browser (especially on mobile phones) has to parse that massive text string into JSON. This is CPU-intensive and will make the app freeze or crash ("Out of Memory" error).

#### C. JSONBin Limits
JSONBin.io (and similar services) usually have strict limits on the size of a single "Bin":
*   **Free Tier:** Often limited to 100KB or 1MB per request.
*   **Paid Tier:** Even paid tiers usually cap requests at 10MB to 20MB.
*   **Result:** The moment you add your 3rd or 4th high-quality image, the `saveData` function will fail with a `413 Payload Too Large` error, and **no data will be saved**.

### 3. The Recommended Solution

The standard industry practice for web apps is **Reference Storage**.

1.  **Host the Image Elsewhere:** Upload the actual image file to a service designed for binary files.
    *   **Cloudinary:** (Excellent free tier, optimizes images automatically).
    *   **Imgur:** (Free, easy API).
    *   **AWS S3 / Firebase Storage:** (Professional standard).
2.  **Store the URL in JSONBin:**
    Only save the link to the image in your JSON data.

**Your Data Structure becomes:**
```json
{
  "id": "p1",
  "name": "Luxury Towel",
  "imageUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/linenlogic/image/upload/v1234/towel_blue.jpg"
}
```

### Summary Table

| Feature | Storing Base64 in JSONBin | Storing URL (Cloudinary/Firebase) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **JSON File Size** | Massive (MBs to GBs) | Tiny (KBs) |
| **App Load Speed** | Very Slow (Laggy) | Fast (Instant) |
| **Mobile Experience** | Will likely crash browser | Smooth |
| **Cost** | Will hit JSONBin limits immediately | Free tiers usually suffice |
| **Offline Support** | Images work offline (if cached) | Images require internet to load first |

### My Recommendation for LinenLogic

Do **not** store images directly in JSONBin.

If you want to add product images:
1.  Add an `imageUrl` string field to your `Product` type.
2.  Manually upload product images to a free host (like Imgur or Cloudinary).
3.  Paste the URL into your product form in the app.
